methods.name: Add(Linetype)
methods.summary: Adds a new linetype with specified definition to the linetype table.

params.name: linetype
params.doc: Definition of new linetype. The information in linetype is copied. If linetype.Name is empty then a unique name of the form "Linetype 01" will be automatically created.

returns.doc: Index of newline type or -1 on error.

methods.name: Add(String, IEnumerable<Double>)
methods.signature: public int Add(string name, IEnumerable<double> segmentLengths)
methods.summary: Adds a new linetype with specified definition to the linetype table.

params.name: name
params.doc: A name for the new linetype.

params.name: segmentLengths
params.doc: Positive values are dashes, negative values are gaps.

returns.doc: Index of new linetype or -1 on error.

methods.name: AddReferenceLinetype
methods.summary: Adds a reference linetypes that will not be saved in files.

params.name: linetype
params.doc: Definition of new linetype. The information in linetype is copied. If linetype.Name is empty then a unique name of the form "Linetype 01" will be automatically created.

returns.doc: Index of new linetype or -1 on error.

methods.name: Clear
methods.summary: Marks all items as deleted.

methods.name: Delete(Linetype)
methods.summary: (Overrides CommonComponentTable<T>.Delete(T).)

params.name: item
params.doc: [Missing <param name="item"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.Tables.LinetypeTable.Delete(Rhino.DocObjects.Linetype)"]

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.Tables.LinetypeTable.Delete(Rhino.DocObjects.Linetype)"]

methods.name: Delete(IEnumerable<Int32>, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Delete(IEnumerable<int> indices, bool quiet)
methods.summary: Deletes multiple linetypes.

params.name: indices
params.doc: An array, a list or any enumerable instance of linetype indices.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: If true, no warning message box appears if a linetype the linetype cannot be deleted because it is the current linetype or it contains active geometry.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded.

methods.name: Delete(Int32, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Delete(int index, bool quiet)
methods.summary: Deletes linetype.

params.name: index
params.doc: zero based index of linetype to delete.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: If true, no warning message box appears if a linetype the linetype cannot be deleted because it is the current linetype or it contains active geometry.

returns.doc: true if successful. false if linetypeIndex is out of range or the linetype cannot be deleted because it is the current linetype or because it linetype is referenced by active geometry.

methods.name: Find(String)
methods.summary: Finds the linetype with a given name.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of the linetype to find. The search ignores case.

returns.doc: If the linetype was found, the linetype index, >=0, is returned. If the linetype was not found, -1 is returned. Note, the linetype index of -1 denotes the default, or "Continuous" linetype.

methods.name: Find(Guid, Boolean)
methods.signature: public int Find(Guid id, bool ignoreDeletedLinetypes)
methods.summary: Finds a linetype with a matching ID.

params.name: id
params.doc: The ID of the line type to be found.

params.name: ignoreDeletedLinetypes
params.doc: If true, deleted linetypes are not checked.

returns.doc: If the linetype was found, the linetype index, >=0, is returned. If the linetype was not found, -1 is returned. Note, the linetype index of -1 denotes the default, or "Continuous" linetype.

methods.name: FindId
methods.summary: Uses the guid to find a model component. Deleted objects cannot be found by id. The guid is the value that is stored in the .Id property. In a single document, no two active objects have the same guid. If an object is replaced with a new object, then the guid persists. For example, if the _Move command moves an object, then the moved object inherits its guid from the starting object. If the Copy command copies an object, then the copy gets a new guid. This guid persists through file saving/opening operations. This function will not find grip objects.

params.name: id
params.doc: ID of model component to search for.

returns.doc: Reference to the rhino object with the objectId or null if no such object could be found.

methods.name: FindIndex
methods.summary: Retrieves a Linetype object based on Index. This search type of search is discouraged. We are moving towards using only IDs for all tables.

params.name: index
params.doc: The index to search for.

returns.doc: A Linetype object, or null if none was found.

methods.name: FindName
methods.summary: Finds the linetype with a given name.

params.name: name
params.doc: he name of the linetype to find.

returns.doc: The linetype. If the linetype was not found, then the default, or or "Continuous" linetype is returned.

methods.name: GetEnumerator
methods.summary: (Overrides CommonComponentTable<T>.GetEnumerator().)

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.Tables.LinetypeTable.GetEnumerator"]

methods.name: GetUnusedLinetypeName()
methods.summary: Gets unused linetype name used as default when creating new linetypes.

returns.doc: The unused linetype name.

methods.name: GetUnusedLinetypeName(Boolean)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("ignoreDeleted is now ignored. Items are removed permanently now. Remove the second method argument.")] public string GetUnusedLinetypeName(bool ignoreDeleted)
methods.summary: Obsolete. Use the other overload. Gets unused linetype name used as default when creating new linetypes.

params.name: ignoreDeleted
params.doc: If this is true then a name used by a deleted linetype is allowed.

returns.doc: The unused linetype name.

methods.name: LinetypeIndexForObject
methods.summary: Returns the effective linetype index to be used to find the linetype definition to draw an object. If an object's linetype source is LinetypeFromObject, the linetype index in the object's attributes is used. If an object's linetype source is LinetypeFromLayer the linetype index from the object's layer is used.

params.name: rhinoObject
params.doc: The Rhino object to use in the query.

returns.doc: The effective linetype index.

methods.name: LoadDefaultLinetypes()
methods.summary: Fills in the linetype table with any default linetypes not already included.
methods.remarks: New documents only contain the continuous linetype. Other default linetypes are added, on demand, when the user needs them. Calling this function ensures that the linetype table is populated with the default linetypes.

returns.doc: The number of default linetypes added to the linetype table.

methods.name: LoadDefaultLinetypes(Boolean)
methods.summary: Fills in the linetype table with any default linetypes not already included.
methods.remarks: New documents only contain the continuous linetype. Other default linetypes are added, on demand, when the user needs them. Calling this function ensures that the linetype table is populated with the default linetypes.

params.name: ignoreDeleted
params.doc: Ignore default linetypes that have been deleted.

returns.doc: The number of default linetypes added to the linetype table.

methods.name: Modify
methods.signature: public bool Modify(Linetype linetype, int index, bool quiet)
methods.summary: Modify linetype settings.

params.name: linetype
params.doc: New linetype settings. This information is copied.

params.name: index
params.doc: Zero based index of linetype to set.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: if true, information message boxes pop up when illegal changes are attempted.

returns.doc: true if successful. false if linetype_index is out of range or the settings attempt to lock or hide the current linetype.

methods.name: SetCurrentLinetypeIndex
methods.signature: public bool SetCurrentLinetypeIndex(int linetypeIndex, bool quiet)
methods.summary: At all times, there is a "current" linetype. Unless otherwise specified, new objects are assigned to the current linetype. The current linetype is never deleted.

params.name: linetypeIndex
params.doc: Value for new current linetype. 0 <= linetypeIndex < LinetypeTable.Count.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: if true, then no warning message box pops up if the current linetype request can't be satisfied.

returns.doc: true if current linetype index successfully set.

methods.name: Undelete
methods.summary: Restores a linetype that has been deleted.

params.name: index
params.doc: A linetype index to be undeleted.

returns.doc: true if successful.

methods.name: UndoModify
methods.summary: If the linetype has been modified and the modification can be undone, then UndoModify() will restore the linetype to its previous state.

params.name: index
params.doc: Zero based index of linetype for which to undo changes.

returns.doc: true if this linetype had been modified and the modifications were undone.

properties.name: ActiveCount
properties.summary: Returns number of linetypes in the linetypes table, excluding deleted linetypes.

properties.name: ByLayerLinetypeName
properties.summary: Returns the text name of the by-layer linetype.

properties.name: ByParentLinetypeName
properties.summary: Returns the text name of the by-parent linetype.

properties.name: ContinuousLinetypeName
properties.summary: Returns the text name of the continuous linetype.

properties.name: Count
properties.summary: Returns number of linetypes in the linetypes table, including deleted linetypes.

properties.name: CurrentLinetype
properties.summary: Returns reference to the current linetype. Note that this reference may become invalid after a call to AddLinetype().
properties.remarks: At all times, there is a "current" linetype. Unless otherwise specified, new objects are assigned to the current linetype. The current linetype is never deleted.

properties.name: CurrentLinetypeIndex
properties.summary: At all times, there is a "current" linetype. Unless otherwise specified, new objects are assigned to the current linetype. If the current linetype source is LinetypeFromLayer the object's layer's linetype is used instead.

properties.name: CurrentLinetypeSource
properties.summary: Source used by an object to determine its current linetype to be used by new objects.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Conceptually, the linetype table is an array of linetypes. The operator[] can be used to get individual linetypes. A linetype is either active or deleted and this state is reported by Linetype.IsDeleted.

properties.name: LinetypeScale
properties.summary: For display in Rhino viewports, the linetypes are scaled by a single scale factor for all viewports. This is not used for printing, where all linetype patterns are scaled to print in their defined size 1:1 on the paper.
